rationale.hitl-workflow: devs supervise, review every change, bounded delegation—few steps at a time
rationale.ai-review: documented practice: agents used as PR/code reviewers
rationale.rules-based-checks: devs validate via tests, linters, version-control rollback
rationale.quality-debt: empirical: AI code messy, over-engineered, poor legacy integration
rationale.agent-scope-drift: agents do more than asked; devs bound steps, reject deps
rationale.survey-input: rich adoption/preference findings on agent task suitability
rationale.counterpoint: opposes vibe-coding/full-autonomy narrative—experts control, don't vibe
rationale.steering: documented practice: prompting strategies, user rules shape generation
rationale.risk-quality: focal treatment: maintainability, readability, correctness of agent code
rationale.risk-overreliance: trust calibration; devs avoid full delegation, review before trusting
rationale.agentic: studies AI agents doing multi-step coding work
rationale.method-field-study: naturalistic observation of developers' own real tasks
